mnemonics: R-E-M-E-M-B-E-R Loops: Repeat Every Method, Especially My Efficient Built-ins, Every Run
keyPoints: Built-in functions like enumerate(), zip(), and range() provide efficient and readable ways to control loops.
These functions eliminate the need for manual counter management, reducing errors and boilerplate code.
Understanding the return types (e.g., enumerate returns an iterator of tuples) is crucial for unpacking values correctly.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to unpack the tuple from enumerate(), leading to 'too many values to unpack' errors.
Using range(len(list)) instead of enumerate() for tracking index and value, which is less Pythonic and readable.
Assuming zip() truncates to the longest list by default; it actually stops at the shortest list unless using itertools.zip_longest.
